label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which statement is true according to the passage. The question is about the writer's memory of locking the front door. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand. The passage starts by talking about memory and how it can be improved with practice. Then it gives two examples of memory tricks. The first example is when the writer found the front door unlocked in the morning, even though they clearly remember locking it the night before. The second example is about remembering not getting a haircut, but then realizing they did get one two days ago. The question is about which statement is true regarding the door locking incident. Let's look at the options: A) One night the writer forgot to lock the front door. B) One night the writer forgot having locked the front door. C) The writer remembered to lock the door. D) The writer remembered unlocking the front door. In the passage, the writer says: "I got up and found that I had left the front door unlocked all night, yet I clearly remember locking it carefully the night before." So, the writer actually did not lock the door (because it was unlocked in the morning), but they remember having locked it. So they have a false memory of locking the door when in fact they didn't. So, the writer forgot to lock the door (they left it unlocked), but they remember having locked it. So the true statement would be A or B? Let's see: A says the writer forgot to lock the door.
